data.title: Brainstorm skips preplanning approval gate when routing MODERATE+HIGH epics to implementation-plan
data.description:
## Problem

When brainstorm Phase 3 Step 4b classifies an epic as MODERATE with scope_certainty HIGH, the routing rule sends it directly to /dso:implementation-plan, bypassing /dso:preplanning entirely. This means no story decomposition, no user approval of the story map, and no adversarial review of cross-story interactions before implementation planning begins.

## How it happened

The brainstorm SKILL.md routing table (Phase 3 Step 4b) says:

| Classification | scope_certainty | Routing |
|---|---|---|
| MODERATE | High | /dso:implementation-plan <epic-id> |

The rationale in the skill file is: "MODERATE+High epics have named files, testable acceptance criteria, and bounded scope — the brainstorm dialogue already produced story-level detail. Preplanning would add overhead without value."

## Why this is wrong

The brainstorm approval gate (Step 4) gets explicit user approval of the epic SPEC — not the implementation PLAN. The user approved:
- The epic's success criteria
- The approach
- The scenario analysis findings

They did NOT approve:
- How the work is decomposed into stories
- The dependency graph between stories
- The walking skeleton / critical path
- The escalation policy for sub-agents

By routing directly to /dso:implementation-plan, the brainstorm skill skipped the preplanning phase where the user reviews and approves the story map. This is a violation of the principle that users approve plans before implementation begins.

In the specific case of dso-qo0x (recipe registry epic), I rationalized this by noting it was "standalone infrastructure with no cross-system integration" and that the scope was clear enough to skip story decomposition. However, this was my judgment substituted for the user's explicit approval — exactly the kind of autonomy that the escalation and approval gates are designed to prevent.

## Expected Behavior

All brainstorm-to-implementation paths should include an explicit user approval of the decomposition plan, regardless of complexity classification. Options:

1. MODERATE+HIGH should route to /dso:preplanning --lightweight (enriches epic with done definitions, presents to user, but doesn't create full story map)
2. MODERATE+HIGH should still route to /dso:preplanning (full mode) — the overhead is justified by the approval gate
3. Add an explicit user confirmation step between brainstorm and implementation-plan for the MODERATE+HIGH path

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Run /dso:brainstorm on an epic
2. Complete Phase 1-3 and approve the epic spec
3. Complexity evaluator returns MODERATE with scope_certainty HIGH
4. Brainstorm routes directly to /dso:implementation-plan without user seeing a story map or approving decomposition
